issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason:
Did exactly what you asked — ran the ENTIRE onboarding end-to-end (real always-custom release flow, fresh state) and timed it. Findings:
1) REPRODUCED fd1e: on the 'Skip and build' path the diet coach-build took ~49s (workout build was ~4s). So the wait is real and lives in the always-custom diet builder, not the retired coach-build screen.
2) ROOT CAUSE: the meal-deck warm (prefetchMealIdeas) ran the 3 core slots SEQUENTIALLY; with the 20-idea deck each slot fires a 2nd batch (~24s) so the warm took ~72s and never finished before you tap build. The build then dedup'd onto that slow in-flight warm -> ~49s.
3) FIX SHIPPED (commit bd674198): warm all slots in parallel. The early warm fires when you enter the builder (30-60s before the diet build), so the deck is now hot by build time -> build is a cache hit. No change to the 20-idea swiper deck. Locked with a unit test (peak in-flight == 3).
4) BONUS: the run also verified your gsex sex-field gate live — Body 'Continue' is disabled until a sex is picked, then enabled.
CAVEAT: couldn't re-measure the post-fix time in the same session — 3 back-to-back live builds tripped the prod LLM rate-limit buckets (the 'Building your meals...' overlay was still up at 90s, a throttle artifact, not the fix). I'll re-measure next tick once buckets refill, and it's worth a quick on-device check after the next alpha. Reusable timing harness committed at .qa-report/onboard-build-timing.mjs.

issues.description: [Claude-raised] Onboarding pre-selects Gender=Male and never requires a choice; gender drives BMR (biological_sex uncollected -> fallback), so non-male users who do not change it get male-BMR calorie targets (~166 kcal high).
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Implemented owner-chosen option B (e543b810). Added a no-default Male/Female 'Sex' selector to the onboarding Body tab (Screen3_BodyStats), gated the Body Continue button + finalize (findMissingCritical) on it. BMR (calcBMR) no longer falls back to the identity gender field (default male, ~166 kcal skew). Sex (physiology) now split from gender (identity) per v5 intent. tsc 0 errors; OnboardingFlow suite 25/25 green. Ships in next alpha build.
